tritonIngest 0.7.1

Does a character vector look numeric-ish (allowing censored notation)?

Source: R/layout.R
is_value_like.Rd

Used by layout detection: a measured-variable column in a wide file contains numbers and possibly censored entries ("<DL", ">2420", "ND", "TNTC").

Usage

is_value_like(x, threshold = 0.8, na_strings = c("-", "--", "n/a", "N/A"))

Arguments

x

Character vector.

threshold

Minimum fraction of non-missing entries that must parse as a number or a recognised censored token.

na_strings

Placeholders that mean "not measured" and are excluded from the denominator alongside NA and "". Excel exports commonly write "-"; before 0.6.0 such a column scored as not value-like and a wide sheet could lose most of its analyte columns.

Value

TRUE/FALSE.

On this page
